text.title: AIDA-Structured Landing Page Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AIDA-Structured Landing Page application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Headline MUST follow [Outcome] + [Timeframe] + [Without Pain]. Generic 'welcome' or product-name headlines are rejected.
rule.rationale: Headline carries ~80% of conversion impact; formulaic outcome headlines outperform creative ones.

rule.statement: Page MUST be structured as Attention / Interest / Desire / Action sections in order. Random-order sections are rejected.
rule.rationale: AIDA structure mirrors decision sequence; out-of-order reads as advertorial.

rule.statement: Each section MUST have exactly one CTA; multi-CTA sections are rejected.
rule.rationale: Multiple CTAs in one section halve click-through; the constraint forces hierarchy.

rule.statement: Social proof element MUST sit below the primary CTA. Above-CTA placement is rejected as decoration.
rule.rationale: Below-CTA placement adds 10-15% CVR; above-CTA placement leaks scroll attention before the ask.

rule.statement: Above-the-fold MUST render the headline + primary CTA on mobile (375px wide). Mobile-broken folds are rejected.
rule.rationale: Mobile traffic is majority; a desktop-only fold breaks the primary conversion on most sessions.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
